siteFooter.description: Mail-in repair for phones, tablets, and laptops with free estimates, human-reviewed quotes, tracked repair progress, and secure return shipping.

home.metaTitle: Repair Center — Premium Mail-In Phone, Tablet & Laptop Repair
home.metaDescription: Mail-in repair for phones, tablets, and laptops with free photo estimates, human review, tracked progress, and secure return shipping.
home.heroTitle: Mail-in device repair you can trust.
home.heroDescription: Repair for phones, tablets, and laptops with a simple estimate process, clear approval steps, and tracked return shipping.
home.devicesIntro: Send in your device — phones, laptops, and tablets — and we will diagnose, repair, and ship it back to you.
home.commonRepairsIntro: Most repairs fall into a handful of categories. Get a price estimate in minutes.
home.deviceCardPhonesText: Screen repairs, batteries, charging ports, cameras, and common hardware issues.
home.deviceCardLaptopsText: Battery service, keyboards, storage upgrades, software recovery, and select hardware repairs.
home.deviceCardTabletsText: Glass and screen repairs, batteries, charging ports, and other common tablet repairs.
home.deviceCardDesktopsText: Handled case by case when the repair and shipping make sense for a mail-in service.
home.repairScreenText: Cracked glass, dead pixels, or touch issues repaired with quality replacement parts.
home.repairBatteryText: Weak, swollen, or fast-draining batteries replaced to restore reliable daily use.
home.repairChargingText: Loose, damaged, or inconsistent charging ports repaired so your device powers properly again.
home.repairKeyboardText: Stuck, damaged, or unresponsive laptop keys repaired with compatible replacement parts.
home.repairSsdText: Upgrade storage for faster performance, more space, and smoother everyday use.
home.repairSoftwareText: Help for devices that will not boot, run poorly, or need a clean operating system reinstall.
home.trustSafeText: Every shipment is tracked and insured so you always know where your device is.
home.trustQualityText: We use vetted replacement parts and provide a warranty on every repair we complete.
home.trustSupportText: Real people review every estimate and answer questions throughout the repair.
home.whatWeRepairTitle: Phones, tablets, laptops, and select desktops
home.readyHeading: Get your repair price in seconds.
home.readyBody: Use our instant estimator to see pricing right away, or submit a full estimate with photos.

howItWorks.title: How mail-in repair works
howItWorks.subtitle: A simple, transparent process from estimate to return shipping.
howItWorks.step1Text: Tell us about your device and the issue. Add photos if you can — we use them to spot extra damage before you ship.
howItWorks.step2Text: A technician reviews your request and sends a firm price. Approve when you are ready and we email shipping instructions.
howItWorks.step3Text: Pack the device using our checklist and ship it to our facility. Tracking is included.
howItWorks.step4Text: We diagnose, repair, and ship the device back. You can track every step from your phone.
howItWorks.heroTitle: A simple mail-in repair process from estimate to return delivery
howItWorks.heroDescription: The process is designed to stay clear and easy to follow from the moment you request an estimate to the day your device is shipped back.
howItWorks.process1Text: Upload photos, choose your device, and describe the issue before mailing anything in.
howItWorks.process2Text: We review the request and send the next step based on the repair details you provided.
howItWorks.process3Text: You only move forward when you are ready to continue with the repair process.
howItWorks.process4Text: Once your device arrives, it is checked in and inspected before the repair moves forward.
howItWorks.process5Text: Your device is repaired, tested, and prepared for return once the work is complete.
howItWorks.process6Text: Your repaired device is shipped back with tracking so you can follow the final step too.
howItWorks.expectHeading: Clear steps before, during, and after the repair
howItWorks.expectBody: You start with an estimate, approve the next step before shipping, follow progress while the repair is underway, and receive tracking when the device is on the way back.
howItWorks.readyBody: That is the first step before anything is mailed in.
howItWorks.heroAlt: Phones, tablets, and laptops arranged on a desk

faq.heroTitle: Answers to the questions customers usually have before mailing in a device
faq.heroBody: We want the process to feel clear from the start, so you know what to expect before, during, and after the repair.
faq.stillNotSureBody: The estimate request is the best first step because it lets us review your device details, photos, and issue description before anything is shipped.
faq.q1: Do I need an account to request an estimate?
faq.a1: No. You can submit a free estimate request without creating an account.
faq.q2: Is the photo estimate always the final price?
faq.a2: No. Photo estimates are preliminary unless clearly marked otherwise. Final pricing can change after in-hand inspection if hidden damage or additional parts are involved.
faq.q3: When do I ship my device?
faq.a3: You only ship your device after reviewing and approving the estimate or next step shown in your repair flow.
faq.q4: Can I track the repair after approval?
faq.a4: Yes. Once your repair moves forward, you can follow status updates, shipment changes, and repair messages from the tracking page.
faq.q5: What if inspection changes the repair scope?
faq.a5: If inspection reveals more damage or different repair needs, a revised estimate can be sent before work continues.
faq.q6: What should I do before mailing my device?
faq.a6: Back up your data, remove any SIM or memory cards if appropriate, and follow the mail-in instructions shown after approval.
faq.q7: Will I approve the repair before work starts?
faq.a7: Yes. The repair should not move forward without your approval of the applicable estimate.
faq.q8: Do you repair every kind of device?
faq.a8: No. We focus on phones, tablets, laptops, and selected jobs that fit a practical mail-in repair process.

repairsPage.heroTitle: Common repairs for the devices people use every day
repairsPage.heroBody: We focus on repair categories that are practical for a mail-in service and easy for customers to start with through the estimate process.
repairsPage.imageAlt: Close-up of an electronic circuit board
repairsPage.noteTitle: Some repairs need inspection before final approval
repairsPage.noteBody: Certain issues, especially liquid damage, no-power problems, or hidden internal damage, may need in-hand inspection before the repair can be fully confirmed.

devicesPage.heroTitle: Browse supported models before requesting an estimate
devicesPage.heroBody: Choose the device that matches yours so the estimate request starts with the right repair options and pricing path.
devicesPage.imageAlt: A laptop, tablet, and phone on a white desk

contact.body: Questions about the platform, your trial, pricing, or anything else — send us an email and we will get back to you within one business day.
contact.generalBody: For questions about RepairCenter, your account, or how the platform works.
contact.salesBody: Not ready to start a trial yet? We are happy to answer questions about whether RepairCenter is the right fit for your shop.
contact.faqItem1: The free trial is 14 days, no credit card required.
contact.faqItem2: You can cancel anytime from your billing page — no contract.
contact.faqItem3: Mail-in and in-store repairs are both supported on all plans.
contact.faqItem4: Stripe is optional — manual payment mode is available.

forShops.metaTitle: RepairCenter for Shops — Run Your Repair Business Online
forShops.metaDescription: Give your repair shop a professional front door. Branded estimate forms, customer tracking, automated payments, and a full admin dashboard. Built with mail-in workflows at the center; designed for shops that handle both mail-in and in-store repairs. Founder Beta plan at ${price}/month with a 14-day free trial.
forShops.heroTitle: Stop managing repairs from your inbox.
forShops.heroBody: RepairCenter gives your shop a professional front door — branded estimate forms, customer tracking, automated payments, and a full admin dashboard. Built for mail-in. Works in-store too.
forShops.heroFinePrint: 14-day trial · No credit card required during the trial · Cancel anytime · Founder Beta at ${price}/month after trial
forShops.painsTitle: Running a repair shop should not feel like this.
forShops.pain1Headline: Customers DM you for a quote. You reply two days later.
forShops.pain1Body: They already went somewhere else. Your repair skills are great. Your intake process is costing you jobs.
forShops.pain2Headline: "Hey, where's my phone?" — answered 11 times this week.
forShops.pain2Body: Each reply interrupts a repair in progress. You're spending technician time doing customer service that should be automated.
forShops.pain3Headline: Venmo. Cash. "I'll pay you when I pick it up."
forShops.pain3Body: You're collecting payment however the customer prefers, with no deposit to hold the job, and no clear record of what was paid and when.
forShops.pain4Headline: Your tracking system is a spreadsheet. Or your memory.
forShops.pain4Body: When a job falls through the cracks, you find out because the customer shows up angry — not because your system caught it first.
forShops.pain5Headline: You sent an estimate. They ghosted. Three weeks later they're furious.
forShops.pain5Body: No follow-up. No reminder. No audit trail. Just a customer who thinks you lost their device and a repair you never started.
forShops.pain6Headline: You have no idea which repairs are actually profitable.
forShops.pain6Body: Screen replacements feel busy but margins are thin. Motherboard jobs pay well but take all day. You're guessing instead of managing.
forShops.featuresTitle: One platform that handles all of it.
forShops.featuresBody: Every feature is a direct answer to a real problem. Nothing bloated. Nothing you will not use.
forShops.feat1Headline: A branded shop page that's actually yours
forShops.feat1Body: Your shop gets its own URL — /shop/your-brand — with your colors, your name, and your estimate form. Not a Facebook page. Not a Google Form. A real shop.
forShops.feat1Kills: Kills: "My shop has no professional presence"
forShops.feat2Headline: Quotes that don't live in your DMs
forShops.feat2Body: Customers submit a repair request with photos right from your page. You review, approve, and send an estimate in one click. The whole thread is logged.
forShops.feat3Body: Every customer gets a live tracking link. They check status, read your messages, and approve estimates — without calling or texting you.
forShops.feat3Kills: Kills: "I answer the same question 10 times a day"
forShops.feat4Headline: Deposits up front. Final balance automated.
forShops.feat4Body: Collect a deposit before you touch the device. When the repair is done, send the final balance request in one click. Stripe handles the rest.
forShops.feat4Kills: Kills: "I collect payment however the customer wants"
forShops.feat5Headline: A dashboard that shows you what's actually happening
forShops.feat5Body: Repair queue, technician assignments, priority flags, SLA tracking, parts inventory, and analytics — all in one place. Know which jobs are overdue before your customer does.
forShops.feat5Kills: Kills: "My tracking system is a spreadsheet"
forShops.feat6Body: Customers can book a drop-off appointment from your shop page. You see it in your dashboard alongside your mail-in queue. One system for both.
forShops.feat6Kills: Kills: "Walk-in customers have no way to book ahead"
forShops.diffTitle: Built with mail-in workflows at the center.
forShops.diffBody1: Most shop-management platforms were designed around a front counter and a cash drawer. Mail-in tends to be a bolt-on.
forShops.diffBody2: We built mail-in first — the estimate flow, the tracking link, the deposit collection, the shipping confirmation — and then designed in-store on top. The result is a platform that fits how remote repair works without stripping out what walk-in shops need.
forShops.setupTitle: Your shop is live in 10 minutes.
forShops.step1Body: Sign up, name your shop, add your branding. Takes about 5 minutes. No technical setup required.
forShops.step2Body: Send customers to /shop/your-brand. Put it in your bio, on a receipt, in a text reply. It's your shop's front door.
forShops.step3Body: Quotes, repairs, payments, messages, parts, and analytics — one admin panel, always up to date.
forShops.pricingTitle: Founder Beta Plan — everything included during beta.
forShops.pricingBody: One transparent price while we are in beta. Public tiers (Starter, Growth, Pro, Advanced) will follow when the product is out of beta. Founder accounts may receive grandfathered terms at our discretion.
forShops.pricingFinePrint: 14-day free trial · No credit card required during trial · Cancel anytime
forShops.compareFootnote: Comparison based on publicly available pricing and feature descriptions as of May 2026. Sourced from each vendor's public website at that date. Features and prices change — check each vendor's site for current information.
forShops.faq1Q: Do I need to be a mail-in shop to use this?
forShops.faq1A: No. RepairCenter is built with mail-in workflows at the center, but the platform handles walk-in repairs, drop-offs, and appointment bookings too. Most shops use both.
forShops.faq2Q: What happens after the free trial?
forShops.faq2A: If you have not added a payment method, your trial ends and access is paused — you will not be charged. If you have added a card, your subscription continues at the current Founder Beta rate of ${price}/month. You can cancel anytime from the billing page; there is no contract.
forShops.faq3A: The Founder Beta plan is ${price}/month while we are in beta and includes every feature in the product today. When we launch public tiers (Starter / Growth / Pro / Advanced) features will be allocated across tiers and prices will change. Founder Beta accounts may be eligible for grandfathered pricing at our discretion — we will give you reasonable notice before any change.
forShops.faq4Q: Can I bring my existing customers over?
forShops.faq4A: New accounts start fresh — RepairCenter builds your history as jobs come in. Your pricing rules are set up during onboarding. If you need to migrate data from another platform, get in touch and we will help.
forShops.faq5Q: Do my customers need to create accounts?
forShops.faq5A: No. Customers can access their repair status via a secure link sent to their email. They can also optionally sign in with a magic link to see all their repairs at your shop in one place.
forShops.faq6Q: Do I need Stripe to accept payments?
forShops.faq6A: Only if you want to accept online card payments through the platform. You can set your shop to manual payment mode and collect cash, Zelle, Cash App, or Square yourself — the platform still handles everything else. Your shop is the merchant of record for repair payments either way.
forShops.faq7Q: Who is responsible for the actual repair?
forShops.faq7A: Your shop is. RepairCenter is software — we provide the intake form, the tracking page, the payment tools, and the admin dashboard. The repair itself, the pricing, the warranty, the refund policy, and the shipping instructions are all controlled by your shop.
forShops.ctaTitle: Your shop could be live in 10 minutes.
forShops.ctaBody: No demo call. No sales rep. Sign up, configure your shop, and share your link. Free for 14 days, then Founder Beta at ${price}/month.

instantEstimate.metaDescription: Get an instant repair price estimate for your phone, tablet, or laptop.
instantEstimate.title: Get your repair price in seconds.
instantEstimate.body: Pick your device and repair type below. No forms, no waiting. See your estimated price instantly.

helpCenter.metaDescription: Find answers about repair tracking, bookings, payments, and managing your repair shop.
helpCenter.heroSubtitle: Search articles or browse by category below.
helpCenter.noArticlesYet: No articles in this category yet.
helpCenter.voteNoAria: No, this article was not helpful
helpCenter.voteThanksNo: 👎 Thanks for the feedback. We'll work on improving this article.

paymentCheckout.depositBody: An inspection deposit is required to begin the repair process. It will be credited toward your final repair bill once the work is complete.
paymentCheckout.emailLabel: Email address used for this quote
paymentCheckout.aboutDeposit1: Pay the deposit to confirm your repair slot.
paymentCheckout.aboutDeposit3: Deposit is credited toward your final bill.
paymentCheckout.noPaymentBody: This repair does not require an upfront deposit. You can proceed directly to the mail-in instructions.
paymentCheckout.alreadyPaidBody: Your deposit has already been received. Proceed to the mail-in instructions to ship your device.
paymentCheckout.depositCreditedNote: The deposit will be credited toward your final repair bill.
paymentCheckout.payDepositBody: Your card will be charged {amount}. This deposit will be credited toward your final repair bill.
paymentCheckout.unableConnect: Unable to connect. Please try again.
paymentCheckout.verifyingBody: This will only take a moment.
paymentCheckout.successDepositBody: Your inspection deposit has been received and your repair order{orderNumber} is now active.
paymentCheckout.shipDeviceBody: Follow the mail-in instructions to package and send your device to us. Include your quote ID {quoteId} inside the package.
paymentCheckout.trackBody: You can check the status of your repair at any time using your quote ID and email address.
paymentCheckout.paymentFailedMsg: Your payment was not completed. Please try again.
paymentCheckout.missingPayInfo: Missing payment information. Please contact support.
paymentCheckout.unableConfirmFull: Unable to confirm payment. Please contact support with your quote ID.
paymentCheckout.errorContactNote: If you were charged and are seeing this message, please contact us with your quote ID and we will look into it right away.
paymentCheckout.errorContactNoteAlt: If you were charged and are seeing this message, please contact us with your quote ID and we will investigate.
paymentCheckout.balanceBody: Once the final balance is paid, the repair can move into final shipping and return delivery.
paymentCheckout.whenCollected1: The repair is marked financially cleared.
paymentCheckout.whenCollected2: Your order can move to final shipping.
paymentCheckout.whenCollected3: You can keep tracking progress from your repair page.
paymentCheckout.noBalanceTitle: This repair does not need another payment
paymentCheckout.noBalanceBody: Your order already appears fully paid. You can return to your tracking page.
paymentCheckout.payBalanceBody: Your card will be charged {amount}. Once payment is confirmed, the repair can move to final shipping.
paymentCheckout.successBalanceBody: Your repair has been financially cleared and can now move toward final shipping.
paymentCheckout.trackReturnBody: Your tracking page will show the next updates as the repair moves into final shipment.
paymentCheckout.whatHappens2: Your order can move to ready-to-ship and outbound tracking.
paymentCheckout.whatHappens3: You can follow all updates from the tracking page.

customerPortal.title: My Repairs
customerPortal.subtitle: Look up your repair history and track active repairs.
customerPortal.errorConnect: Unable to connect. Please try again.
customerPortal.feedbackTitle: Send feedback or report an issue
customerPortal.feedbackTypeFeatureDesc: I'd like to see something new or improved
customerPortal.feedbackMessagePlaceholder: Describe the issue, idea, or feedback in detail…
customerPortal.feedbackErrorSubmit: Failed to submit. Please try again.
customerPortal.feedbackThanksBody: We review every submission and use it to improve the product.

invite.title: Join {orgName}
invite.signinFirst: Sign in or create an account, then click Accept below.
invite.errExpired: This invitation has expired. Ask an admin to resend it.
invite.errUsed: This invitation has already been used.

errors.notFoundText: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has moved.
errors.serverErrorText: We've been notified and will fix it. Please try again in a moment.

legal.translationDisclaimer: This translation is provided for convenience. The English version is the authoritative legal text. In case of conflict, the English version prevails.

email.footerNote: This is an automated message from {orgName}. Questions? Reply to this email.
email.platformFooterNote: This is an automated message from Repair Center. Need help? Reply to this email.
email.finalBalanceHeading: Time to pay the final balance
email.finalBalancePaidSubject: Payment received — your repair will be returned shortly
email.trialWarningHeading: Your free trial is ending soon

adminAnalytics.title: Analytics Dashboard
adminAnalytics.subtitle: Revenue, conversions, repairs, and device trends.

adminSla.title: SLA & Turnaround
adminSla.allOnTrackSubtitle: No overdue or stuck orders right now.

adminReviews.title: Customer Reviews
adminReviews.searchPlaceholder: Search by customer, device, or comment…

adminStaff.title: Staff performance
adminStaff.subtitle: Technician workload, repair throughput, and average turnaround for the last 30 days.

adminPricing.title: Pricing Rules
adminPricing.intro: Edit the public price shown to customers for each device and repair type. Set to Manual review to hide pricing and always require a custom quote.
adminPricing.errorLoadCatalog: Failed to load catalog. Please try again.
adminPricing.errorSelectModelAndType: Please select a device model and repair type.
adminPricing.confirmDelete: Delete this pricing rule? This cannot be undone.
adminPricing.noActiveRulesWarning: No pricing rules are currently active. Customers will see every repair as requiring manual review. Activate at least one rule or set prices to make them visible.
adminPricing.duplicateWarning: A rule for this combination already exists. Edit it in the list below.
adminPricing.noMatching: No pricing rules match this filter.

adminQuoteDetail.estimateBuilderIntro: Move this reviewed quote into the estimate builder to create line items, shipping, discount logic, and send a real estimate record to the customer flow.
adminQuoteDetail.depositPaidNotice: Deposit has been marked as paid.
adminQuoteDetail.mailInPlaceholder: This link becomes active after the estimate is approved.
adminQuoteDetail.operationsIntro: Once the device is approved and mailed in, use the repair order page to update intake, inspection, repair progress, and return shipment tracking.
adminQuoteDetail.pricingRuleEmpty: No pricing rule is linked to this quote request yet.

adminEstimateBuilder.revisedLoadFailed: Unable to load revised estimate data.
adminEstimateBuilder.successCreated: Estimate {id} created and quote moved to estimate_sent.
adminEstimateBuilder.mailInPlaceholder: This link becomes active after the customer approves the estimate.
adminEstimateBuilder.customerVisibleNotesPlaceholder: What the customer should know about this estimate.
adminEstimateBuilder.revisedCustomerVisibleNotesPlaceholder: Example: During inspection we found additional board-level damage that changes the repair total.
adminEstimateBuilder.internalNotesPlaceholder: Supplier, margin, or repair planning notes.
adminEstimateBuilder.revisedInternalNotesPlaceholder: Internal repair findings and quote rationale.
adminEstimateBuilder.confirmSendTitle: Confirm: Send this estimate to the customer?
adminEstimateBuilder.confirmSendBody: This will email the estimate to {recipient} and change the quote status to estimate_sent.
adminEstimateBuilder.confirmRevisedSendTitle: Confirm: Send this revised estimate to the customer?
adminEstimateBuilder.confirmRevisedSendBody: This will email the revised estimate to {recipient} and set the order to awaiting final approval.

adminCustomers.title: Customers
adminCustomers.subtitle: All customers who have submitted a repair request.
adminCustomers.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, email, or phone…
adminCustomers.emptyState: No customers yet. Customers appear here when they submit a repair estimate through your shop page.

adminTeam.inviteLinkLabel: Invite link (share with the new team member):

adminPayments.title: Payments · Order #{order}
adminPayments.finalBalanceIntro: Use this action when the repair is complete and the customer needs to pay the remaining balance before return shipping.
adminPayments.policyTrackingValue: Customer can follow status from the tracking page
adminPayments.summaryUnavailable: Payment data is not available yet.

adminRepairOrder.customerNotePlaceholder: Example: Device received and checked into intake.
adminRepairOrder.technicianNotePlaceholder: Example: Backlight circuit damaged. Will need board-level repair in addition to screen replacement.
adminRepairOrder.beyondRepairTitle: Beyond economical repair — ship device back
adminRepairOrder.noFaultTitle: No fault found — ship device back
adminRepairOrder.beyondRepairBody: The repair cost exceeds the value of the device. Record the return shipment details below and notify the customer.
adminRepairOrder.noFaultBody: No defect was found. Record the return shipment details below and notify the customer.
adminRepairOrder.unrepairedBody: The customer declined the revised estimate. Record the return shipment details below.
adminRepairOrder.finalApprovalTitle: Use when intake changes the quote
adminRepairOrder.finalApprovalBody: If inspection reveals extra damage, hidden defects, or additional labor, send a revised estimate and move the repair into final customer approval.
adminRepairOrder.intakeIntro: Record the device state when it arrives. This protects against disputes about pre-existing damage.
adminRepairOrder.intakeNotesPlaceholder: Any additional observations about the device on arrival.
adminRepairOrder.messagesIntro: Staff notes and customer-facing messages. Mark internal-only to keep a note visible only to staff.
adminRepairOrder.messagesNewPlaceholder: Type a message or internal note…
adminRepairOrder.messagesErrorMarkRead: Unable to mark messages as read.
adminRepairOrder.staffNotesIntro: Private notes visible only to staff — not shared with the customer.
adminRepairOrder.staffNotesPlaceholder: Add internal notes about this repair…

adminParts.heading: Parts Catalog
adminParts.intro: Track parts inventory and costs used in repairs.
adminParts.lowStockAlertOne: {count} part is below the low-stock threshold.
adminParts.lowStockAlertOther: {count} parts are below the low-stock threshold.
adminParts.emptyAddFirst: No parts yet. Add your first part above.

adminCatalog.heading: Repair Catalog
adminCatalog.intro: View the global device catalog and add custom brands, models, and repair types for your shop. Custom items are available alongside global ones when creating pricing rules.
adminCatalog.globalCustomNotice: Global items are maintained by the platform and are read-only. Custom items belong to your shop — you can edit or delete them.
adminCatalog.confirmDeleteBrand: Delete this brand? This will also remove its models and any linked pricing rules.
adminCatalog.confirmDeleteModel: Delete this model? Pricing rules for this model will also be removed.
adminCatalog.confirmDeleteRepairType: Delete this repair type? Pricing rules using it will also be removed.

adminWalkin.heading: Walk-in intake
adminWalkin.intro: Create a repair order for a customer who has arrived in person.
adminWalkin.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, phone, or email…

adminAppointments.heading: Appointments
adminAppointments.intro: Manage drop-off appointment requests from customers.
adminAppointments.truncatedNotice: Showing the first 500 appointments. Export or filter by date range to see older records.
adminAppointments.confirmConvert: Convert this appointment to a walk-in repair order?
adminAppointments.calendarEmpty: No appointments in the visible window.
